title: Quamoclit hastigera
intro: Quamoclit hastigera, commonly known as the Cypress Vine or Star Glory, is a flowering vine native to the Americas, characterized by its bright orange, star-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Quamoclit hastigera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Quamoclit hastigera is classified in the family Convolvulaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as bindweeds and morning glories. This plant belongs to the genus Quamoclit, which consists of only one species, Quamoclit hastigera. The species Quamoclit hastigera is further classified as a member of the order Solanales. It is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas and the Indian subcontinent. Quamoclit hastigera is also known as the "Climbing Morning Glory" or "Scarlet Glory".
